title: Lemon Herb Chicken
description: Bright, easy sheet-pan dinner with roasted vegetables.
steps: Heat oven to 425°F.
Halve potatoes and onion; toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
Nestle chicken among vegetables. Add garlic, lemon slices, and oregano.
Roast 30–35 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F.

title: Salmon Rice Bowl
description: Flaky salmon over rice with cucumber and a quick soy-lime sauce.
steps: Season salmon with salt and pepper; pan-sear 4 minutes per side.
Slice cucumber and green onion.
Whisk soy sauce, vinegar, honey, and lime juice.
Serve salmon over rice with cucumber; drizzle sauce and top with sesame.

title: Veggie Pasta Primavera
description: Colorful weeknight pasta with whatever vegetables are in the fridge.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (zucchini, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes)
steps: Boil pasta in salted water until al dente; reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Sauté garlic and vegetables in olive oil until tender.
Toss pasta with vegetables, parmesan, and pasta water as needed.
Finish with basil and red pepper flakes.

title: Weeknight Turkey Chili
description: Hearty, make-ahead chili that tastes even better the next day.
steps: Brown turkey with diced onion in a large pot.
Stir in spices, tomatoes, beans, and broth.
Simmer 25–30 minutes until thickened.
Season to taste; serve with toppings of choice.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in spiced tomato sauce — great for brunch or dinner.
steps: Sauté onion, pepper, and garlic until soft.
Add tomatoes, cumin, and paprika; simmer 10 minutes.
Make wells in the sauce and crack in eggs.
Cover and cook until whites set; top with feta and cilantro.

title: Ginger Chicken Stir-Fry
description: Fast stir-fry with a savory ginger-garlic sauce.
steps: Stir-fry chicken in vegetable oil until nearly cooked; set aside.
Sauté ginger, garlic, and vegetables until crisp-tender.
Return chicken; add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Serve over rice.
